pr-evidence/post513-strict-bundle-snapsh.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer reported no actionable findings. The reviewer verified README.md, docs/v1-requirements-matrix.md, and tests/test_project_governance.py; confirmed the wording matches the local post-#513 strict bundle manifest with ok=true and artifact_count=37; confirmed the docs preserve benchmarks/ as human-facing benchmark evidence and pr-evidence/ as PR traceability; and found no claim that this PR launched new Databricks runs.
verification: poetry build -f wheel -> built cachet-kv 0.2.0 wheel

poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.release_bundle ... --require-complete-v1 --output-dir databricks-runs/current-governance/complete-strict-release-bundle-with-g5-post-513-current-wheel --output-json databricks-runs/current-governance/complete-strict-release-bundle-with-g5-post-513-current-wheel.json --overwrite -> ok=true, 37 artifacts
GPT-5.5 reviewer ran poetry run pytest -q tests/test_project_governance.py -> 50 passed
what_changed: Updated README.md and docs/v1-requirements-matrix.md from the stale post-#511 34-artifact strict-bundle snapshot to the verified post-#513 37-artifact snapshot.

Recorded that the refreshed snapshot carries PR #512/#513 traceability evidence and the new dependency_freshness strict sidecar while preserving the existing g6/L4, g5/A10G, storage, SGLang live V1, and native-engine benchmark evidence references.
Updated project-governance tests so old 34-artifact snapshot wording is treated as stale and the post-#513 37-artifact wording is pinned.
pr-evidence/post513-strict-bundle-snapsh.why: PR #513 made dependency freshness a strict release-bundle artifact, so the release-readiness docs need to cite the current strict-bundle validation state instead of saying dependency freshness belongs only in a future refresh.
